element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Free training allows players to learn pitch controls before competitive play
Dedicated training modes for free kick and corner kick execution before scenarios
16 formations and 8 strategies selectable before friendly matches
12 historical match recreations with varying time pressure and deficit/lead conditions
Knockout and league formats with increasing difficulty across rounds
Multiple weather conditions and 8 stadiums with different pitch materials and dimensions
48-team league with home and away matches, longest endurance challenge

typical_tension_triggers: Opponent ball possession in attacking third
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-based rhythm with discrete tension peaks during set pieces and final minutes; no in-match continuous tension curve—tension resets between matches in tournament modes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Training modes and friendly matches with adjustable goalkeeper skill and player handicap allow skill acquisition without penalty
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Short tournaments and regional qualifying rounds introduce knockout pressure while maintaining team selection flexibility
difficulty_curve.late_game: World Series (48 teams, home-away format) and scenario mode comebacks demand sustained consistency and mastery of set pieces under time pressure
notable_spikes: Scenario 1 (Italy vs Croatia, 1:14 remaining) — tight time window with deficit
Scenario 2 (Germany vs Bulgaria, 2-3 deficit) — trailing position amplifies pressure
Tournament finals — single-elimination match determines progression
World Series depth — 48-team round-robin requires endurance across many matches

notable_level_patterns.description: Player inherits losing or tied match position with limited time remaining (0:42 to 1:54 minutes). Must either equalize or overtake opponent within constraints. Historically-inspired scenarios (Italy vs Croatia, Germany vs Bulgaria, England vs Brazil, etc.)

Single or double-elimination format where advancement requires consecutive wins. Short Tournament (64 teams) and International Cup (World Cup format with regional qualifiers) escalate opponent difficulty through rounds
notable_level_patterns.example_level: International Cup with regional qualifying round to final

notable_level_patterns.description: World Series mode spans 48 teams in home-and-away format (longest sustained play structure). No knockout pressure but requires consistency across many matches
Challenge mode decouples specific mechanics (penalty kicks, free kicks, corners, general pitch control) into separate practice contexts before applying them in live match scenarios
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Training: Penalty Kicks, Free Kick Training, Corner Kick Training
